transcript.clean_text: a hot sunny day on the coast greeted the giro d'italia pelaton after their rest day on stage nine at jaya hindley who took the victory blockhouse the scene of the first big gc sort out juanpe lopez had managed to hold on to the pink jersey despite initial doubts a 12-second lead still held over joao almeida in an iberian one-two with frenchman roman balde in third place carapaz and hindley completing the top five simon yates had seemingly dropped off the gc losing 11 minutes but he'd carry on in the race 166 riders remained nobody failing to start smiles and a different approach for some an opportunity for the sprinters as they look to go up the coast and into the hills but would it be the pure sprinters or would it be the likes of matthias von de pool would a big break get away it would certainly be an interesting two halves to the stage flat as a pancake for the first hundred kilometers and then into the marquee and its famous moody finishing after a climb to monsano in yeezy the race would also pass through philotrano hometown of the late micheli scarapony it took around 15 kilometers for the break to go properly and when it did go three men would be in it lawrence nassen alessandro de marquis and matthias and how he could have tried on a couple of occasions and several other riders thought about getting up the road once things were established they were allowed to take a lead a bit of laughing and joking and poking fun back in the peloton all good natured friends after arguments just a couple of days before lot of sudal alpacin fenix and anto marshay would control once we got to the intermediate sprint the gap would be just under five minutes using the most energy to go out there and get them he'd be followed behind by demar give my degents and gaviria the hills we go then anta marsha is still working alpacine working with them lotto stood all no longer because caleb yuen was the first of the sprinters to be dropped that's 76k still from the finish a few kilometers later mark cavadish would start to lose contact as well that's your fault suffering mechanical issues inside the final 60 k's an impressive chase back on and he'd soon be back in business the race approached philotrano as they honored michele escarponi taken from us all five years ago a brilliant presence in the peloton a top bike rider and friend to many intermediate sprint though was of no consequence in the race alessandro de marquis chose to go it alone after that but with the attacks behind the chase on with 20 k's to go he would be caught one more classified climb remained and it was getting very very hot at the front difficult to control as they approached that final climb to be as foss had to go his attack didn't last too long one kilometer in fact he was brought back and then it was a turn of the likes of the general classification riders to have a go corvy would also throw his hat into the ring and arnold de mar started to lose contact just inside the final kilometer of the climb domenico botsovigo pulled the latest attack back for binyam kilmai who almost took a wrong turn six kilometers to go and simon yates was off the front followed by formula fonder pool and chicorni but nobody will be working with mateo fonda pool and despite his frustrated attempts to get away the bunch would soon reel him in richard carapaz darting to the front once more hugh carthy also having a go he'd be caught inside the final kilometer domenico potsovivo of all people starting the lead out binyam germai hitting the front another duel with fonder pool just like on day one this time history was made veni vidi bini binyam gemai becoming the first ever eritrean the first ever black african to win ajido the italian stage the thousands of eritrean supporters who turn up at bike races finally had something to cheer about the flag waved over the finish line the man he beat giving the thumbs up a perfect image binyam gilmai from kentucky to the giro d'italia a natural-born superstar gideomai beating fonder pool albanesek kelderman and canopaz ander marche working all day long and gil mai with a great victory juanpe lopez survived to take another day in the pink jersey gilmar went off to get all the congratulations and the general classification as it was the evening before juanpe lopez still leading by 12 seconds over joao almeida roman balder still at 14. it should be the same after stage 11. not too many banana skins around a flat stage and one that should be for the sprinters just over 300 meters of climbing over 203 kilometers a stage without any king of the mountains points two intermediate sprints all the way to reggio emilia fernando gaviria won the last time we were in red to emilia five years previous how's it going to go this time remember you can watch the general italia and all of its history making moments on gcm plus discovery plus and eurosport you
